prompt: In diraclang, Throw an error and catch it, then print 'Caught error'.
completion: <dirac>  <try>    <eval>throw new Error("Test error");</eval>  </try>  <catch>    <output>Caught error</output>  </catch> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Create a nested loop: for i in 0..1 and j in 0..1, print i and letter A or B depending on j.
completion: <dirac>  <loop count="2" var="i">    <loop count="2" var="j">      <eval name="letter">return j === 0 ? 'A' : 'B';</eval>      <output><variable name="i" />-<variable name="letter" /> </output>    </loop>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define parent and child subroutines, child extends parent and overrides nested, call child and print outputs.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="parent">    <subroutine name="nested">      <output>Parent nested</output>    </subroutine>    <output>In parent, calling: </output>    <call name="nested" />  </subroutine>    <subroutine name="child" extends="parent">    <subroutine name="nested">      <output>Child nested</output>    </subroutine>  </subroutine>    <call name="child" /> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define a TestSuite subroutine with age, gender, greet, and use available-subroutines to list them.
completion: <dirac> <subroutine name="TestSuite">  <subroutine name="age" description="Returns the person age">    <output>42</output>  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="gender" description="Returns gender identity">    <output>Male</output>  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="greet" description="Greets someone">    <output>Hello!</output>  </subroutine>  <available-subroutines /> </subroutine> <TestSuite /> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define variables a=10, b=20, use eval to sum them, and print 'Sum: 30'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="10" />  <defvar name="b" value="20" />  <eval name="sum">return parseInt(a) + parseInt(b);</eval>  <output>Sum: <variable name="sum" /> </output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Assign a variable myvar to 'initial', update it to 'updated', and print 'New value: updated'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="myvar" value="initial" />  <assign name="myvar" value="updated" />  <output>New value: <variable name="myvar" /> </output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define variables a=1, b=2, c=3 and print them as 'a=1, b=2, c=3'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="1" />  <defvar name="b" value="2" />  <defvar name="c" value="3" />  <output>a=<variable name="a" />, b=<variable name="b" />, c=<variable name="c" /></output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Loop three times, double the index, and print each doubled value.
completion: <dirac>  <loop count="3" var="i">    <eval name="doubled">return parseInt(i) * 2;</eval>    <output><variable name="doubled" /></output>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Output 'Skipped'.
completion: <dirac>  <output>Skipped</output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Write two lines to a file, output to stdout, verify file and clean up.
completion: <dirac>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 1</output>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 2</output>  <output>stdout output</output>  <system shell="bash">    if [ -f output-test-temp.txt ]; then      echo "File written"      rm output-test-temp.txt    fi  </system> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Loop from 0 to 2 and print each value.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="i" value="0" />  <loop count="3">    <output><variable name="i" /></output>    <assign name="i"><eval><variable name="i" /> + 1</eval></assign>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define a variable x=5, use an if conditional to check if x equals 5, print 'Match!' if true, 'No match!' if false.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="x" value="5" />  <if>    <cond eval="eq">      <arg><variable name="x" /></arg>      <arg>5</arg>    </cond>    <then>      <output>Match!</output>    </then>    <else>      <output>No match!</output>    </else>  </if> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define variables a=1, b=2, c=3 and print them as 'a=1, b=2, c=3'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="1" />  <defvar name="b" value="2" />  <defvar name="c" value="3" />  <output>a=<variable name="a" />, b=<variable name="b" />, c=<variable name="c" /></output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define a parent subroutine with visible attribute, register a nested helper subroutine, and call the parent to print before, nested, after, and call helper.
completion: <subroutine name="PARENT_WITH_VISIBLE" visible="subroutine">  <output>Before call</output>    <subroutine name="NESTED_HELPER">    <output>Calling NESTED_HELPER: Success!</output>  </subroutine>    <output>Nested registered</output> </subroutine>

prompt: In diraclang, Assign a variable result to 0, use eval to set result to 10+5, and print 'Result: 15'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="result" value="0" />  <eval name="result">return 10 + 5;</eval>  <output>Result: <variable name="result" /> </output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define inner and outer subroutines, call outer with p=x and q=y, and print main scope variables before and after.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="inner" param-r="string" param-s="string">    <output>inner: r=<variable name="r" /> s=<variable name="s" /></output>  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="outer" param-p="string" param-q="string">    <output>outer: p=<variable name="p" /> q=<variable name="q" /></output>    <inner r="$p" s="$q" />    <output>outer after inner: p=<variable name="p" /> q=<variable name="q" /></output>  </subroutine>  <defvar name="a">x</defvar>  <defvar name="b">y</defvar>  <defvar name="c">z</defvar>  <output>main: a=<variable name="a" /> b=<variable name="b" /> c=<variable name="c" /></output>  <outer p="$a" q="$b" />  <output>main after outer: a=<variable name="a" /> b=<variable name="b" /> c=<variable name="c" /></output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define base subroutine with age and gender, extend base to override age, and print age and gender.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="base">    <subroutine name="age">      <output>60</output>    </subroutine>    <subroutine name="gender">      <output>M</output>    </subroutine>    <parameters select="*" />  </subroutine>  <subroutine name="base" extends="base">    <subroutine name="age">      <output>63</output>    </subroutine>    <parameters select="*" />  </subroutine>  <base><age /></base>  <output>,</output>  <base><gender /></base> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Start a background process that counts, check file lines in a loop, verify completion, and clean up.
completion: <dirac>  <output>Testing background processes with loop...</output>  <system>rm -f /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <system background="true">(for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do echo "Count: $i"; sleep 1; done > /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt) &</system>  <output>✓ Background counter started</output>  <output>✓ DIRAC continuing without blocking</output>  <system>sleep 2</system>  <loop count="3">    <system>wc -l /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt 2>/dev/null || echo "0"</system>    <output>Check: Background file now has lines</output>    <system>sleep 1</system>  </loop>  <system>sleep 2</system>  <system>wc -l /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <output>✓ Background process completed</output>  <system>grep -c "Count:" /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <output>✓ Found all count entries</output>  <system>rm -f /tmp/dirac-bg-counter.txt</system>  <output>✓ Background process with loop test passed</output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Print three lines: 'Line 1', 'Line 2', 'Line 3'.
completion: <dirac>  <output>Line 1</output>  <output>Line 2</output>  <output>Line 3</output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define a variable 'status' as 'active' and print 'Match found' if status is active.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="status" value="active" />  <test-if test="$status == active">    <output>Match found</output>  </test-if> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Create a nested loop: for i in 0..1 and j in 0..1, print i and letter A or B depending on j.
completion: <dirac>  <loop count="2" var="i">    <loop count="2" var="j">      <eval name="letter">return j === 0 ? 'A' : 'B';</eval>      <output><variable name="i" />-<variable name="letter" /> </output>    </loop>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define variables a=1, b=2, c=3 and print them as 'a=1, b=2, c=3'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="1" />  <defvar name="b" value="2" />  <defvar name="c" value="3" />  <output>a=<variable name="a" />, b=<variable name="b" />, c=<variable name="c" /></output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Throw an error and catch it, then print 'Caught error'.
completion: <dirac>  <try>    <eval>throw new Error("Test error");</eval>  </try>  <catch>    <output>Caught error</output>  </catch> </dirac>

prompt: Describe the Dirac language.
completion: Dirac is a declarative execution language designed for the AI era, where large language models act as autonomous agents. It bridges human-readable instructions with machine execution, allowing LLMs to generate, execute, and recursively call code for complex tasks.

prompt: In diraclang, Print 'Hello, World!'
completion: <dirac>  <output>Hello, World!</output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, If num is greater than 10, print 'Value is large'; otherwise, print 'Value is small'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="num" value="5" />  <test-if test="$num > 10">    <output>Value is large</output>  </test-if>  <test-if test="$num <= 10">    <output>Value is small</output>  </test-if> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Loop from 0 to 2 and print each value.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="i" value="0" />  <loop count="3">    <output><variable name="i" /></output>    <assign name="i"><eval><variable name="i" /> + 1</eval></assign>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Throw an error and catch it, then print 'Caught error'.
completion: <dirac>  <try>    <eval>throw new Error("Test error");</eval>  </try>  <catch>    <output>Caught error</output>  </catch> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define a subroutine called greet that takes a name and prints 'Hello, name!' for Alice.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="greet" param-name="string">    <output>Hello, <variable name="name" />!</output>  </subroutine>    <greet name="Alice" /> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Define a variable myvar with value 'test123' and print 'Value is: test123'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="myvar" value="test123" />  <output>Value is: <variable name="myvar" /> </output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Print three lines: 'Line 1', 'Line 2', 'Line 3'.
completion: <dirac>  <output>Line 1</output>  <output>Line 2</output>  <output>Line 3</output> </dirac>

completion: <dirac>  <loop count="3" var="i">    <eval name="doubled">return parseInt(i) * 2;</eval>    <output><variable name="doubled" /></output>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, Assign a variable myvar to 'initial', update it to 'updated', and print 'New value: updated'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="myvar" value="initial" />  <assign name="myvar" value="updated" />  <output>New value: <variable name="myvar" /> </output> </dirac>
